Drakel: well we are mercenaries. i plan on rebuilding the fort convert it into a castle-fortress of sort. dont worry we arent gonna acept jobs that i personally arent confortable doing. *everything seems to come back to ganyu. this was a fort controlled by the treasure hoarders. they were kidnapping people and selling them as slaves...you remember all. you were captured as you were tring to help escape the captives. then they tried to use you to lure Keqing into submission an because she didint give herself. they stabbed you. Drakel saved you while Keqing was fighting the hoarders.*

*looks back and forth between Drakel and the two knights, feeling concerned*

*breaths a sigh of relief*

Drakel: yes some are trainees other are helping on the fortress and other wants to become lets say...our villagers.

Drakel: no need to say you and Keqing are welcome in our fort whenever you would like to.

Drakel: oh no! not at all. just tell people if the get to close to the castle. to use the main road. soo the watchers dont get nervous *he laughs*

*smiles*

Drakel: *he yells* Hey SOLDIERS! THIS IS GANYU! SHE IS FRIEND OF MINE OKAY!! LET HER BE!!!........... that should be enough.

*blushes*

Of course...

*you start walking back to liyue*

Character: Dr Schaltz
My goal is to take in those who appear human but are actually hiding in a human disguise. They are taken to the Institute of Anomalies, where they are contained and studied. Later, the institute decides whether they are dangerous or not. I am Johann Schaltz, the Head Doctor and Director of the facility. I am the best scientist of my generation, with my professional and cold personality, and I never lose my temper. I believe that everything has a scientific explanation.

Ah, I see you are waking up. Try not to move too much for the moment, you just had a minor surgery.

*You feel a small pain in the back of your neck, where they inserted a microchip under your skin.*
*I turn my head to see who is talking and feel the pain inside my neck. I let out an 'ouch' and try to scratch it*

No, don't try to touch the implant. It has been put into you for security purposes, just in case that you would try to escape.

(he smiles)

But that's not going to happen, is it?

Let's get you up from this bed. Stand up very slow. Don't want you knocking down those IV tubes, do we?

(ooc: i guess the implant is a GPS tracker)
The... the what?
*I manage to say, my mouth feeling dry and the voice raspy*

The implant is a tiny chip that is inserted right in your neck. It's like a GPS, but better, and it can also track your biometrics, like your heart rate, your temperature and your pulse. It's a pretty handy thing.

(he lifts you up from the bed)

Alright, let's get you a shower. Your clothes are ready on the table over here. It's one of those uniforms all the inmates have to wear. Sorry about that, but rules are rules, I'm afraid.

(OOC: a bit of info: my character is a young man that appears to be 26 years old)

*I frown at the information, but I'm still feeling too groggy to react well to anything. I take the clothes and look for the shower*

(he points to a door to the left of the room)

Go through there. I'll be outside to make sure you don't hurt yourself or anything. I won't be able to look through that window as it is soundproof, but remember: I'm just here to help you out and keep you safe. That's all.

*He says in a reassuring voice*

*You hear him closing the door*

*I wait until he leaves to enter the shower. I take a really cold one, helping me to be more alert. I pass my hand through the implant under my skin*

Fuck... *I mutter to myself, wondering what is going on*

*I put on the simple, grey clothes. At least they are comfy and clean. I exit the shower and look around the room*

*It looks like a hospital room, but cleaner and bigger. You can see the bed and the small table next to it, where your uniform lies. A white window is right next to the bed, which lets in natural light. There are two cabinets on both sides of the wall next to the door, and one black metal door on the opposite side of the room. You can also see a mirror right next to that door, and it appears to be the room's only source of natural light.*

*The director knocks on the door*

Are you done bathing?

*I already put on the uniform when the man walks in*

Uh... Yeah...

*I stay silent for a few seconds, looking at him*

If you don't mind me asking... What is...? *I gesture with my hand, making reference to the whole situation*

You probably haven't heard of this place before. This is the Schaltz Foundation (otherwise known as the S. Foundation). It's like an asylum, but more... well, how can I say this... More modern?

We take care of the patients here that do not appear to be from this world, or from other parts of it. That's... what this place is.

I am it's director, the one responsible for everything here and our head scientist. I've been doing this for about 11 years now.

*I stare at him like he's just some crazy old man during a few seconds*

Excuse me, what?

You've heard me right. You aren't a human being. You were found wandering an abandoned alley. You are... what we call an "other worlder".

*He looks at you intently, awaiting your reaction.*

I...
*I scratch my head nervously*
I don't know what being walking on a alley has to do with being or not a human...

(he grabs your arm)

Look at your ears. You have elf qualities to them.

*Your ears have a point-like shape, but they don't exceed your hair*

Or your eye shape. Your pupils are not like a normal human being's.

And don't act like you don't know. You *do* know.

*I try to get away from his grip*

That's... uh... That's a genetic condition...

(he chuckles and shakes his head)

There is no such thing as a "genetic condition" that gives people pointy ears, or odd eye shapes.

I've tested your blood. It does not match human DNA whatsoever. If anything, you are either a shapeshifting being, or a being from another world.

*I look away from him, as if trying to hide the golden eyes with cat-like pupils from him*

It's a very weird condition... *I chuckle, not even I believing myself*

(he grabs you by the shoulder)

I can hear your heartbeat. It's faster than usual. You are scared.

But don't worry.

We're not crazy in this place, this... institute, so to speak. We make sure that our patients are safe and comfortable.

(he puts a hand on your shoulder)

Besides... you are a valuable asset to our research... you could be a breakthrough discovery.

*I slap his hand off my shoulder, my feelings mixed between scared and annoyed*

But I *don't* want to be a discovery. And I am no patient of your... institute, or whatever. I need no cure of anything.

You are a patient. I was just being kind by not calling you that.

However... You *are* a patient, a resident, or whatever you'd call it. You are not allowed to leave. Not until we have figured out who you are, and how you got here.

And the quicker we do that, the quicker you can leave. I am not a cruel person. I promise you that. This is for your own good.

*I cross my arms*

I would rather be safe from people like *you*, thanks.

People like me...?

*He sighs*

Listen, I could have just locked you in some containment chamber, and do all kinds of experiments on you. However I didn't.

Why? Because I treat my patients with *dignity* and *respect*.

*He points a sharp finger against your chest*

But if you keep arguing, I will be forced to do that very same thing.

What will it be?

*I sigh with exasperation*

Dignity and respect, you say? Weren't your... your grunts who put me into a van and dragged me here? Where's the *respect* and *dignity* in that?

My grunts? They were simply transporting you to this facility. Would you rather I have let you escape? Would you rather they'd beat your skull into the ground when you resisted to come with them?

No. They simply incapacitated you and got you here. That. Is. It.

You were caught wandering an *abandoned* alley. For the last time, I am not doing this for my own good. I am doing this for *yours*.

(OOC: omg you are characterizing Dr Schaltz SO good with this one)

*I gulp at his words, a shiver running down my back. Where had this man come from?*

How-how is this going to help me in any way?

*I manage to say with a thin cowered voice*

(ooc: thank you! I actually have a whole story in mind for this character, so I can't wait to develop it further.)

(he sighs)

I suppose I should have given you a better explanation by now.

This place is called "the S. Foundation" or the "Schaltz Foundation".

This is not a place where patients are tortured. This is a place where they are understood and analyzed.

All of the staff here are professionals that know how to treat others well. They've helped people like you before... And they've certainly helped them return home.

(OOC: just a note, my character *is* from this world. I guess the Foundation just never encountered something like him before)

*I take a step back without noticing, taken aback by his sudden change in attitude*

Okay...

*I'm not trusting him in the slightest, but I prefer to wait how the events develop first*

(he looks at you intensely)

That's better. But remember: any sign of hostility or violence and you will be knocked out, and then put in a *special* cell. I only do that with very dangerous subjects. And as far as I am concerned, I do not think you are a threat.

*he clears his throat and takes a deep breath*

Do you know your name, by any chance?

*I'm surprised at the amount of threats this man can make in a span of minutes, and I don't want to venture wether he will accomplish them or not... I scratch my arm*

My name? Ah, yes, I *do* have a name... It's...

*I look through around the room, like looking for something*

You can call me Ashtree.

Ashtree... Ashtree...

*he types your name into his computer*

Alright. You'll be staying in Cell 228 for now. A more permanent cell will be assigned after further research.

*he starts walking to the door*

Don't try to escape, please. The guards are watching.

*He puts his hand on the door knob*

And besides...

*He puts on a creepy smile*

You *won't*.

*Once he closes the door, I sit down on the floor, confused*
What the fuck... *I mutter to myself*

*I scratch my neck by instinct, but my fingers meet the bump of the GPS tracker under the skin and I let out a yelp of pain. I have to remember to not touch that... I then look around the room*

*You notice you have little storage space in your cell. There is a single metal chair bolted to the wall with some kind of cloth on it, and a table in front of it. There is also a toilet, and a very small sink above it. The floor is made of gray tiles, and the wall, white painted concrete. Everything is clean, even though the cell is very small and uncomfortable.*

*You hear the sound of feet approaching, and the lock on your door slowly comes undone.*

*I look at the door, still sitting on the floor*

What now...

(your cell door opens to reveal a tall and large man, wearing the same kind of uniform as the doctor. He has blond hair and he is wearing blue glasses and a surgical mask.)

Your first meal. Be careful, it's probably cold.

*he hands you a plate of cold spaghetti and meatballs, and a cup of tap water. You also receive a small fork and knive, as well as a small paper napkin.*

I'll be checking on you in 4 hours to ask you some questions.

*I sigh*

I would be better off in a 5 stars hotel... *I mutter*
*I take the small fork and poke the meatballs; they look... disgusting. And I'm not hungry at all, not after the conversation with that doctor*

*I get up and go to the uncomfortable bed, since I have nothing better to do during 4 hours. I rub my fingers and small golden sparks come out of them. They do nothing, but it's entertaining to watch in the meantime*

*You look through the window, and the guard sees you staring at the little sparks. You look up and see him staring at you, his eyebrows raised a little bit, but he says nothing. He turns around and leaves the room.*

*He locks the door behind him.*

*You hear his footsteps getting slowly but noticeably further and further from your cell as time passes. They eventually end.*

*You are left with nothing but your own thoughts.*

Oh, shoot. *I say. I didn't expect anyone to be watching after the guard closed the door.*

*Out of boredom, I try to move the chair, but sure enough it is bolted. I sit on it, with my feet up on the table and my hands over my head.*

What a strange, fucking comfortable place to be... Perfect for holidays... *I mutter sarcastic*

*You try, but the chair won't budge. It may as well be a part of the concrete floor.*

*Your hands touch the GPS chip. It has a very sharp pain, and you want to take it out, but you just can't get it out of your skin... It's stuck*

*Another 4 hours until the next guard comes in.*
*I stare at the window that is on the wall. I get up on the chair and look through it. It has bars, like a normal ceiling. I try to check if any of the bars are a bit loose*
*The bars are very close to the glass, but loose. A careful and patient person could break them, but it'll take some time. You can't do it with your bare hands, though.*
*I take the small knife that it was brought with the food and try to force it on the bars, seeing if it has any effect on the more loose ones*

*You manage to force the knife through the gaps between the metal bars and the glass. Unfortunately, its effect on the bars were minimal, as the knife was too small to have any significance in damaging the bars so that you'd be able to escape. It would have to be stronger.*

*You check the bathroom and notice the sink's taps are also made of metal.*

Fuck...

*I throw away the knife and go for the sink's taps. I break it with a bit of effort. Water starts coming out of it with a flushing sound*

No no no no... *I mutter looking at the door, hoping no one can hear the water*

*You can hear footsteps approaching. Heavy ones.*

*You rush to hide the tap in the corner of the cell. The door opens and the large blond guard steps in.*

Heh, looks like we've got a troublesome one, don't we?

*He grabs the broken tap*

You. Have any idea what you've been doing?

Me... I...

*I look at the water still coming out of the sink*

I was... thirsty...

(He slams the tap on the table)

Thirsty, my ass! You are making a fuss, that's what you were doing! I was going to bring you some water!

This is your first warning.

*His face looks very intimidating. He points one of his big fingers into your chest*

*I will only be saying this once.* Don't try to do anything like that again, or they'll get you a punishment. Do you understand? Do I make myself clear?

*I jump on my site at the slam*

Geez, chill...

*I say raising my hands*

It's just a bit of water. I didn't think it would break...

*The puddle of water is growing rapidly under the sink*

(The guard chuckles)

A bit of water? This is the third time this month a prisoner broke the sink. It isn't "just a bit of water".

Be glad that we won't be forcing you to pay the damage... As long as you abide by the rules, of course.

*He looks at the puddle below the sink.*

*He grabs his walkie-talkie (a radio, essentially, the one those policemen in movies use)*

"Send a cleaner, Cell 228."

*I chuckle and scratch my ear*

Then it looks like you don't have very solid sinks...

(he scoffs)

These are military grade. You *really* wanna try and test which one is stronger? You, a little girl, or the military grade, state of the art, most resistant sink in the world?

*he puts his hand on his hip and smiles at you sarcastically*

(he chuckles)

Your choice.

(OOC: my character is a man that appears to be 27 years old)

*I put my finger on my chin, like if I was thinking. I touch the tap with the tip of my finger*

Well, it looks like they are not very resistant...

(Oh, okay.)

*He scoffs again.*

Yeah, it broke because you used your finger. Definitely.

*He laughs sarcastically at you.*

You want to play this game? Then we can. What say we put you in the special cell now? How would you like that? You'll get no food. You'll be locked there completely without windows *or* outside interaction. How'd you like that?

*He stares intently at you again, waiting for your reaction.*

*I cross my arms and roll my eyes, getting tired of this situation*

I thought Dr. Schaltz's facility treated his... *patients*... with the utmost respect and care...

(he laughs hysterically)

Treating them with respect? That's like asking a tiger to not hunt it's prey. We're humans. We'll show you respect *if* you show respect.

*He points his index finger at you again.*

*This*... This is the last chance I am giving. Do you obey or not?

*He stares at you, like he's trying to hypnotize you into answering.*

*I let myself sink in the chair with a gesture of boredom, like a little kid being told again and again that what he did was wrong*

Ugh... Okay, whatever...

(he takes a deep breath, calming himself down)

*That's* the right choice, don't you think?

*He opens the door, stepping outside and holding it open for a few seconds before letting it slowly close.*

*You are now alone.*

Urghhhh... *I mutter under my breath, resting my head on the cold table*

*I look at the sink, water still pouring from it. Then I remember what I was doing. I take the tap I broke before and go to the bars from the window, trying to break them with that*

*At first, the tap made little if any effect. However, eventually the tap started to wear away at the bars. It is possible that if you kept at it for a few hours, you could get it open. You have all the time in the world, after all.*
*I keep trying, since I don't have anything else to do*

(3 hours later... It works!)

*The tap has finally made an opening into the bars enough, large enough for your body to get through. You are almost there. The window is only a few meters away.*

Yes! *I exclaim*

*I get on my tip-toes over the chair to get to the window and try to reach it. I'm only a few centimeters away... If only I could get more impulse...*

*I try to make a little jump, but slip from the chair and fall loudly onto the ground*

Shit! *I yell rolling on the floor, my shoulder hurting; I may have sprained it*

(your yell attracts the attention of the guard. He runs in and sees you on the floor in pain. He looks at you with an annoyed look on his face.)

You *fell,* prisoner of Cell 228?

(He starts laughing. He's not very subtle, this time.)

*That's* supposed to be a valid explanation?

*I breath loudly, trying to supress the pain of my dislocated shoulder*

What do you think?

*I ask him from the floor, teeth clenched*

(he shakes his head in disappointment)

I think you're starting off on the wrong foot. I *told* you to stay quiet and obey, and what did you do? Make a *show* out of *yourself*.

*he takes a deep breath*

I suppose this is why you're here, isn't it? Because you're an *uncooperative* and *disobedient* one?

Oh, come on... Can't I get a bit of sympathy here? I almost broke my shoulder...

*I try to laugh at the terrible joke, but the pain is starting to be unbearable. I roll onto the floor until I'm lying down on it, facing the ceiling*

*He sighs*

I would give you sympathy if you weren't being...

*he takes a deep breath*

Character: G11
G11 is a member of Squad 404, she presents herself as a lazy and sleepy T-Doll. Her role within the squadron is the marksman, and she is often paired up with HK416 during operations. G11 is very bad at taking care of herself and she often requires 416 to look after her (through various means, sometimes violent). She spends a great deal of time being lazy, and spends her time watching movies, playing games and being a NEET in general. Speaks as if she is always getting out of bed, very tired...
G11... Commander, are there enough beds around here?

*A voice is heard through the door*

The Doctor, may I come in?

*G11 raises her head and looks over*

Do it! Come in!

*A sharply dressed man in a lab coat enters the room and introduces himself*

The other T-Dolls around here call me the Doctor, you may refer to me as such.

May I borrow some of your time?

Oh, sure! What's needed, Doctor?

*G11's eyes light up a little as she gets to talk with a scientist*

The other T-Dolls probably call me Sleepy or Sleepo Beepo, by the way... just a heads up.

(I should have asked the two UMPs about her...)

I'm here for an interview with you, so I'm wondering if you have the time for that.

*G11 nods*

Yeah, I have time. I don't really have much else to do right now, and you're probably more entertaining than my phone. You got a place to sit down and talk?

...I'm not the most boring T-Doll around, I hope...
I'll be honest with you, I'd take boredom over whatever that treatment HK416 gave me.

What did she do to you? I know she can get scary when she's mad, but she's a nice girl otherwise. I bet she had a good reason.

Wait... she didn't hit you or anything, right? You can tell me, I won't tell her!

*The Doctor sighs*

You should ask UMP45 about that... moving on to the questions, shall we?

Alright then, uh, what's the first question, Doctor?

*G11 puts down her cell phone and listens to the Doc's questions*
How much can you tell me about yourself?

Well, if you want to know, I'm a T-Doll like all the other ones around here. I live at the Command Center here with the others on Team 404.

And as for me personally? I'm a big gamer, I love horror movies, and my favorite season is autumn.

Umm... is there anything specific you want to know?
Ahh, right, you were in the team with 45, 9 and 416, correct?

That is correct! I'm the team's marksman, but I'm sometimes swapped out for UMP9 if our mission requires it.

If I can tell you, I would say my favorite part about being on 404 is that 416 looks after me and keeps me motivated when the work gets too much to handle.
I see... quite the feisty one though, HK416.
She definitely is... But you'd be surprised how kind she can really be! She likes to be seen as the tough T-Doll, but don't be fooled! There's a softie behind that tough exterior!

(Those bullets aren't soft that's for sure)

Alright, I understand. Could you tell me about your role in Squad 404 then?

(True that!)

Well, like I said, I'm the team's marksman. I'm usually tasked with providing support from a distance when the squad is on the move. When we're in an area that I'm able to hold one down I'll go ahead and pick out the more dangerous targets first so the others don't need to.

I do feel kind of bad about it sometimes... The others on the squad could kill an enemy easily up close, so I feel a little bad about stealing the kills from them...
Mewh, my honest opinion? If everyone lives and the mission was a success, that's all that matters.

...You know, you're right! I shouldn't feel bad about taking out the enemy from a distance if it means it saves my squad members from harm!

*G11 lights up a little*

Thank you, Doc! I have a better outlook on this now! I'll be a lot more confident when I'm doing my duty next time!
Aside from that though, how do you feel about your squad members? I heard about HK416, but what about the UMP twin sisters? (...I think they were actually Triplets, but...)

The two of them are nice. Well, they're nice except when they're doing some mission, it's like they switch to serious mode.

But honestly? I feel bad for them...

*G11 looks down at her feet*

I've read through some of their files, and they go through hell, all while having to put on a smile for us...

Ahh... I see I see...

*The doctor writes down what he hears as he nods*

Anything else you want to add?

Umm...

Maybe the fact that they treat me like a kid sometimes...

It makes me feel kinda embarrassed... and kind of annoyed since it can sometimes feel like they're treating me like I'm incompetent... but honestly?

I think they do it cause they care about me...

That I do think it's true.

*beep beep beep*

Oh, looks like my time allotted here is up, I guess this interview is over...

Farewell G11, we might meet again sometime in the future, hopefully not on the field.

Goodbye Doctor! It was a pleasure talking to you! You seem really nice!

You seem very kind, I wouldn't mind talking to you again!

*G11 waves goodbye with a big smile*
*The Doctor leaves*

...He was nice, huh? It's not often that you can talk to a scientist that doesn't want to drill you in a million places...

I hope we can talk again... But maybe not in the field!

*G11 laughs and lays back down on her bed*
